data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with this child (S4 Attested signing)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). This child is the signing/attestation slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. Empty G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads working tree; all read snapshot at pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it consumes the pinned SHA but does not own the re-rooting. Not an absence (S1/S3 cover it).
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible re-run over identical content) — FULLY covered by THIS child (its AC1: attested PASS records verified_at_sha on the ticket, signs over it, re-run reproduces a verdict over identical content), with the immutable-SHA basis from S1. This is the heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — the "cannot sign" half FULLY covered by THIS child (its AC2: a local/dirty run never emits a signature, verify-and-report only); the "reads in-place checkout" half covered by S3 (its AC2). Split is clean; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates, no git-lock contention / cross-op corruption; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar, no hardcoded /tmp, flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by existence of all six children (this child is story #4 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative (every gate verifies a client-specified immutable reproducible snapshot; signed verdicts reproducible + bound to verified_at_sha; unsigned local preserves verify-before-push) decomposes onto AC1-AC3. The "signed verdicts are reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable (its AC1/AC3, in-toto Statement envelope) and the "unsigned local preserves verify-before-push" clause is reinforced by this child's signing bar (its AC2). No uncovered obligation introduced.

Note: this child's AC5 (HMAC trust-model docs) is explicitly delegated to S6 ("delivered via S6"); S6 AC4 names that obligation — covered-by-named-consumer, not an absence.

Note (scope, not a G3 finding): the existing G4 review on this child flags a seam/dependency-gap around attest.py routing between S3 and this child. That is a consistency (G4) concern about WHO threads the snapshot handle into the plan-review hashing path — not a G3 coverage gap, since parent AC2/AC4-coherence is still CLAIMED by a child (this one). G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the signing/attestation slice of the parent criteria; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
